inbody.height_note: Height not in CSV; retained from WHOOP / prior profile (1.86 m).
context_notes: Dylan reports no structured gym training for ~2–3 months prior to this scan.
Compare to Oct/Nov 2025 scans in history.json — clear drop in SMM with concurrent fat loss.

notes: Average recovery over last 60 days is solid (>=67).
Average sleep duration supports progressive overload.
Average daily cycle strain ~8.55 over the window.

body.note: Height retained from WHOOP (not in InBody CSV). Weight/composition from InBody 2026-07-16.

goals.primary: Look better with a stronger, more defined upper body
goals.notes: Upper-body bias locked for Plan C block. Visual: bigger shoulders, more definition, less belly. Legs maintenance only.

trial_block.note: Dylan opted into hard push for remaining block; InBody re-eval 2026-08-08.

status.label: detrained_restart
status.note: No structured training for ~2–3 months; plan design deferred until source packs exist.

philosophy: Maximum results in 3 gym days/week (experiment through InBody)
Progressive hypertrophy RT — upper bias, stable menu
Hard sets near failure (0–2 RIR); progress load/reps weekly
Fewer longer gym sessions over many short ones
Home fallbacks only if gym impossible

gym.equipment: Full commercial gym (free weights, machines, cables, cardio)

nutrition.self_assessment: Mostly under control; not asking for a full diet overhaul
suspects: Carbs may be low outside breakfast
nutrition.creatine: 5 g/day when training (paused during gym lapse; resume with restart)
nutrition.tracking: No perpetual food logging — spot-checks only if needed for calibration

sports_optional: Paddle — not reliable enough to prescribe

history_timeline.note: Gym membership after cleaning up junk-food eating; somewhat consistent
Restart with friend; researched splits; consistency hard under schedule pressure
No structured training ~2–3 months; occasional home work historically; creatine paused
InBody baseline ingested; source-pack strategy next — plan still locked

measurement.inbody: Baseline on file (2026-07-16); prefer ~monthly — cadence pending sourced guidance
measurement.home_scale: TBD usefulness after sourced retest guidance

today.local_hint: Open Today on your phone at the gym. Gym first. Home only if gym is impossible.

guided_workout.reps_in_reserve: Leave about 0 to 2 reps in reserve. Isolation (laterals, curls, flies) can finish at 0–1. Stop if form breaks.
guided_workout.rir: Leave about 0 to 2 reps in reserve. Isolation (laterals, curls, flies) can finish at 0–1. Stop if form breaks.
guided_workout.load_cue: Work up to challenging working weights. Last hard sets should feel hard — leave ~1–2 clean reps on compounds, 0–1 on laterals/arms.
guided_workout.title: Pull · back + rear delts + arms
guided_workout.focus: Lats, upper back, rear delts, biceps · hinge maintenance
guided_workout.summary: Week 1 · MAX · hard restart · about 74 minutes · Leave about 0 to 2 reps in reserve. Isolation (laterals, curls, flies) can finish at 0–1. Stop if form breaks.

gym.name: Lat pulldown or assisted pull-up
gym.notes: Full stretch at top; pull elbows down. Last set hard.
gym.where: Lat-pulldown station, assisted pull-up machine, or a pull-up bar.
gym.how_to: Pulldown: start with a long reach, pull elbows toward your hips, bar to upper chest, control the return. Assisted pull-up: same idea with help from the knee pad. Negatives: get the chin over the bar, lower for 3-5 seconds.
gym.avoid: Pulling the bar behind the neck. Huge swinging half-reps.
set_plan_lines: Set 1: pin / load for 8–10 with 2–3 left
Set 2: same or +1 pin / +5 kg if clean
Set 3: full stretch at the top every rep
gym.set_plan: Set 1: pin / load for 8–10 with 2–3 left | Set 2: same or +1 pin / +5 kg if clean | Set 3: full stretch at the top every rep

rom_cues.question: Bar to chest or behind neck?
rom_cues.answer: To upper chest. Never behind the neck. Think elbows to hips.
rom_cues.why: Behind-neck is riskier; full stretch at top lengthens lats for the V-taper.

tips: think "elbows to hips," not "hands to bar."
full stretch at the top every rep.

tips_ranked.text: think "elbows to hips," not "hands to bar."
full stretch at the top every rep.

gym.research_note: Back volume supports posture and the V-taper that makes shoulders look wider.
pin_guidance: +1 pin or +5 kg if stack jumps by 5 and form holds

gym.name: Seated cable row
gym.notes: Chest proud; squeeze shoulder blades. Progressive load.
gym.where: Seated cable row with a V-handle or straight bar.
gym.how_to: Sit tall with a slight knee bend. Start with arms long. Pull the handle to your torso, squeeze the shoulder blades, do not shrug. Return to a full stretch. Keep the torso mostly still.
gym.avoid: Rocking with the lower back. Shrugging the traps every rep.
set_plan_lines: Set 1: load for 8–10
Set 2: same or +2.5–5 kg if clean
Set 3: squeeze blades 1 sec — no rock
gym.set_plan: Set 1: load for 8–10 | Set 2: same or +2.5–5 kg if clean | Set 3: squeeze blades 1 sec — no rock

rom_cues.question: Full stretch at the start?
rom_cues.answer: Yes — arms long, then pull to torso. Chest proud, no shrug.
rom_cues.why: Rows balance pressing days and protect shoulders while you bias push for visuals.

tips: chest proud, shoulders down.
squeeze at the chest for one second.

tips_ranked.text: chest proud, shoulders down.
squeeze at the chest for one second.

gym.research_note: Rows balance pressing days and help shoulder health with your pressing volume.
pin_guidance: Progress +1 pin when top of range is easy

gym.name: Rear delt fly (dumbbell, cable, or machine)
gym.notes: Definition + shoulder health.
gym.where: Bent-over dumbbells, reverse pec-deck, or cables.
gym.how_to: Soft elbows. Sweep arms out and slightly back - show the rear of the shoulders to the wall behind you. Light weight; feel the burn.
gym.avoid: Turning it into a heavy shrug or row with swing.
set_plan_lines: Set 1: light load for 12–15
Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg
Set 3: if you only feel traps, go lighter
gym.set_plan: Set 1: light load for 12–15 | Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg | Set 3: if you only feel traps, go lighter

rom_cues.question: Heavy row swing OK?
rom_cues.answer: No. Soft elbows, sweep out/back — show rear delts to the wall behind you.
rom_cues.why: Rear delts round the 3D shoulder; swing turns it into a trap shrug.

tips: this rounds out the shoulder from the back - important for the 3D look.
if you only feel traps, go lighter.

tips_ranked.text: this rounds out the shoulder from the back - important for the 3D look.
if you only feel traps, go lighter.

gym.research_note: Rear-delt work supports aesthetics and pressing comfort.

gym.name: Face pull (cable rope)
gym.notes: External rotation at the end.
gym.where: Cable set at upper-chest / face height with a rope.
gym.how_to: Pull the rope toward your face / forehead. At the end, rotate so thumbs point roughly toward your ears. Squeeze rear delts and upper back.
gym.avoid: Pulling the rope to the belly (that becomes a regular row).
set_plan_lines: Set 1: light pin for 12–15 smooth
Set 2: same pin — thumbs toward ears at the end
Set 3: form first — this is prehab + shape
gym.set_plan: Set 1: light pin for 12–15 smooth | Set 2: same pin — thumbs toward ears at the end | Set 3: form first — this is prehab + shape

rom_cues.question: Pull to belly or face?
rom_cues.answer: To face / forehead. Externally rotate at the end (thumbs ~ears).
rom_cues.why: Belly height becomes a row; face + rotation hits rear delts and external rotators.

tips: this is shoulder-friendly "prehab" and shape work - form first.
external rotation at the end matters.

tips_ranked.text: this is shoulder-friendly "prehab" and shape work - form first.
external rotation at the end matters.

gym.research_note: Helpful accessory for shoulder longevity while you bias pressing for visuals.

gym.name: Dumbbell or EZ-bar curl
gym.notes: No swing. Last set 0–1 RIR.
gym.how_to: Elbows near your sides. Curl up without swinging the hips. Lower fully. Brief squeeze at the top.
set_plan_lines: Set 1: load for 10–12
Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg
Set 3: full lower — no cheat swing
gym.set_plan: Set 1: load for 10–12 | Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg | Set 3: full lower — no cheat swing

rom_cues.question: Skip the bottom stretch?
rom_cues.answer: No — lower fully, brief squeeze at top. Elbows near sides.
rom_cues.why: Cutting ROM chops biceps stimulus; swing is ego, not growth.

tips: lower all the way - skipping the bottom chops the set short.
if you swing, drop the weight.

tips_ranked.text: lower all the way - skipping the bottom chops the set short.
if you swing, drop the weight.

gym.research_note: Direct arm work is optional volume on top of rows/pulldowns.

gym.name: Romanian deadlift or lying leg curl
gym.notes: No swing. Last set 0–1 RIR.
gym.where: Barbell or dumbbell Romanian deadlift, or lying/seated leg-curl machine.
gym.how_to: Romanian deadlift: soft knees, push hips back, weights close to the legs, feel hamstrings stretch, stand by driving hips forward. Leg curl: pad on Achilles, curl heels toward glutes, control down. Light-moderate - hinge maintenance.
gym.avoid: Rounding the upper back hard. Yanking the leg curl.
set_plan_lines: Set 1: load for 8–12 (maintenance)
Set 2: same load — not a max deadlift day
Set 3: hips back, weights close to legs
gym.set_plan: Set 1: load for 8–12 (maintenance) | Set 2: same load — not a max deadlift day | Set 3: hips back, weights close to legs

rom_cues.question: Round the back to go deeper?
rom_cues.answer: No. Soft knees, hips back, flat-ish back, feel hamstrings. Leg curl: control down.
rom_cues.why: Posterior-chain floor for health while upper body stays the priority.

tips: hips go back like closing a car door with your hips.
this is not a max deadlift day.

tips_ranked.text: hips go back like closing a car door with your hips.
this is not a max deadlift day.

gym.research_note: Keeps posterior-chain coverage for the WHO-style major-muscle floor while upper body stays the priority.

gym.name: Easy conditioning finisher
gym.notes: 12 min · Incline walk preferred (less grip fatigue) OR easy StairMaster · zone-2.
gym.where: Stair climber, incline treadmill, or jump-rope space.
gym.how_to: Easy-moderate pace where you can still talk in short sentences. Stair climber: light hands on the rails, full steps. Incline walk: about 5-8% incline, brisk walk not a sprint. Jump rope: steady rhythm. Use the minutes shown on the card.
gym.avoid: All-out intervals that trash tomorrow's lift. Hanging on the stair climber rails and cheating the steps.
set_plan_lines: One easy block for the minutes listed — no kilograms
StairMaster Technogym level ~4–6 (provisional week 1)
Or incline walk 5–8% · talk-test OK
gym.set_plan: One easy block for the minutes listed — no kilograms | StairMaster Technogym level ~4–6 (provisional week 1) | Or incline walk 5–8% · talk-test OK

rom_cues.question: All-out intervals?
rom_cues.answer: No. Easy–moderate zone 2. Short sentences while moving.
rom_cues.why: Protect tomorrow’s lift; recomp support comes from consistency + protein, not punishing cardio.

tips: this supports conditioning and midsection goals; lifting still builds the shoulders.
if you are exhausted from lifting, shorten this, do not skip the whole gym day.

tips_ranked.text: this supports conditioning and midsection goals; lifting still builds the shoulders.
if you are exhausted from lifting, shorten this, do not skip the whole gym day.

gym.research_note: Extra easy cardio is optional support. Your main belly/definition lever in this 3-week restart is lifting + protein + not hard-cutting, not punishing cardio.
gym.stairmaster_note: Technogym / Virgin Active provisional. Light hands on rails, full steps.

target_hr.label: 135–148 bpm
target_hr.note: Provisional zone-2 from your WHOOP resting HR — use talk-test if the watch disagrees.

habits.hydration: Drink consistently; pale urine — more on training days / heat
habits.eating_frame: Maintenance or small surplus on training days (eat enough to train hard). Do NOT hard-cut — BF% already ~16.9%. Soft midsection improves from muscle + consistency (packs 03/05).

guided_workout.missed_day_rule: Priority: (1) Gym on the planned Mon/Wed/Fri. (2) If you miss one, still aim for 3 gym days that week on other weekdays. (3) Home fallback only if gym is impossible. Weekends only if that is the only way to keep a gym session.
attendance_priority: First prize: Gym on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday
Second: Still three gym days — makeup Tuesday or Thursday if you miss a plan day
Last resort: Home only if getting to Virgin Active is truly impossible for that session

session_tips.title: What 'hard' means today
session_tips.body: Max results ≠ sloppy grinding. Last hard sets should leave about 0–2 clean reps (pack 04). If you could do 5 more easy reps, the set was too light.

session_tips.title: Why 3 days still wins for you
session_tips.body: Weekly hard sets drive hypertrophy more than training more days (Schoenfeld frequency metas, pack 02). Pack volume into 3 long sessions; 4–5× historically backfires for you.

session_tips.title: Body-type training pick
session_tips.body: Your InBody profile (lean, lost muscle, upper-aesthetic goal) maps to progressive hypertrophy RT with upper specialization + leg maintenance — not cardio-first or random machine hopping (packs 01/03/04).

session_tips.title: Food around training
session_tips.body: Hit ~120–135 g protein today. Small meal 1–2 h before helps hard sessions. Creatine 5 g daily (pack 05/06).

session_tips.title: Weights in kilograms
session_tips.body: Write every working weight. Next session: same reps + more kg, or same kg + more reps.

guided_workout.session_sources_note: Plan C cites Wave-1 packs 01–07 in data/sources/.

plan.headline: Plan C · 3×75–90 min · MAX RESULTS experiment → InBody Aug 8

block.label: Plan C — 3-week MAX RESULTS experiment (upper hypertrophy)
preference_notes: MAX RESULTS mode: full weekly volume, near-failure effort on hard sets
Upper-body visual priority: shoulders, chest, arms, less belly
Legs maintenance only (WHO floor — not zero)
Training type locked: progressive hypertrophy RT (stable menu) — not HIIT-primary, not 4–5× frequency
Experiment: push hard for 3 weeks then InBody 2026-08-08

visual_primary: Less belly / softer midsection (recomp, not crash cut)

conservative.percent_body_fat: hold or −0.3 to −0.8 pp (may be within BIA noise)
conservative.smm_kg: hold or +0.2 to +0.5 (small; 3 weeks is short)
conservative.note: Conservative = process + small composition drift in the right direction

optimistic.shoulders_subjective: Visible rounder delts in mirror / shirts
optimistic.note: Optimistic = best-case if adherence + protein + sleep hold; InBody noise still applies (pack 07)

process_non_negotiable: Hit all 3 gym days (makeup weekdays OK)
Protein ~115–130 g most days (push toward 130 on training days)
Hard sets finish ~0–2 RIR (isolation can touch 0; compounds keep ~1–2)
Log loads in kg — add weight/reps when top of range is clean

daily_habits.hydration: Drink consistently; pale urine — more on training days / heat
daily_habits.eating_frame: Maintenance or small surplus on training days (eat enough to train hard). Do NOT hard-cut — BF% already ~16.9%. Soft midsection improves from muscle + consistency (packs 03/05).

1.label: MAX · hard restart
1.load_cue: Work up to challenging working weights. Last hard sets should feel hard — leave ~1–2 clean reps on compounds, 0–1 on laterals/arms.
1.reps_in_reserve: Leave about 0 to 2 reps in reserve. Isolation (laterals, curls, flies) can finish at 0–1. Stop if form breaks.

2.label: MAX · build
2.load_cue: Beat week 1: same reps + more kg, or same kg + more reps. No empty sets.
2.reps_in_reserve: Leave about 0 to 2 reps in reserve on hard sets.

3.label: MAX · peak before InBody
3.load_cue: Best loads of the block. Still controlled eccentrics — intensity ≠ sloppy.
3.reps_in_reserve: Leave about 0 to 1 rep on isolation; about 1–2 on big compounds.

push.title: Push · chest + shoulders
push.focus: Chest, front/side delts, triceps · small leg maintenance

gym.name: Incline dumbbell or barbell press
gym.notes: 45° incline. Control the eccentric.

gym.name: Seated dumbbell or machine shoulder press
gym.notes: Don't shrug. Full lockout without slamming.

gym.name: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
gym.notes: Visual priority — soft elbows, lead with elbows. Last set near failure.

gym.name: Cable or machine chest fly
gym.notes: Squeeze at the inside; no ego weight.

gym.name: Cable triceps pushdown (rope or bar)
gym.notes: Elbows pinned.

gym.name: Leg press or goblet squat (or walking lunges)
gym.notes: Maintenance only — hard but leave ~2 clean reps.

gym.name: Easy conditioning finisher
gym.notes: MIX: StairMaster OR incline walk OR jump rope · steady zone-2. Do not turn into a HIIT wreck.

home.name: Push-up (hands elevated or standard)
home.notes: Feet on floor; elevate hands to make harder as needed.

home.name: Pike push-up (shoulder focus)
home.notes: Hips high; head toward floor between hands.

home.name: Side-lying or standing arm-raise holds
home.notes: Slow laterals with water bottles / backpack if you have no dumbbells.

home.name: Easy conditioning finisher
home.notes: Same minutes as gym finisher for this week.

gym.name: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
gym.notes: MAIN visual builder — rest 60–90s. Last 2 sets near failure.

gym.notes: Upper chest carryover to shoulder look.

gym.name: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
gym.notes: Pump / constant tension; take near failure.

gym.notes: Arms finish — near failure on last set.

home.name: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise

schedule.note: Makeup: Wed skipped (girlfriend birthday) → pull on Thu. MAX RESULTS: full volume, near-failure hard sets (experiment mode).
MAX RESULTS shoulders day — priority side-delt volume + hard finishers.

plan.missed_day_rule: Priority: (1) Gym on the planned Mon/Wed/Fri. (2) If you miss one, still aim for 3 gym days that week on other weekdays. (3) Home fallback only if gym is impossible. Weekends only if that is the only way to keep a gym session.
notes: MAX RESULTS experiment locked: 3 gym days/week, hard sessions, 2026-07-20 → 2026-08-07, InBody 2026-08-08.

Training type (body-matched): progressive hypertrophy resistance training on a stable menu — best fit for tall-lean recomp + SMM regain (InBody 71.1 kg, BF% 16.9, SMM −3.2 kg vs Oct). Not HIIT-primary, not pure powerlifting, not 4–5× frequency.
Week 1 exception: Wed pull → Thu 2026-07-23; Fri shoulders. Still 3 gym days.
From Thu onward: set_scale 1.0+ (full hard sets). Leave ease-in behind — Dylan opted into push mode.
Citations: data/sources/01–07 (detraining regain, 2–3× frequency, upper bias, overload/RIR, protein, creatine, retest).
Creatine 5 g daily · protein ~120–135 g · no hard cut.
Open portal Today → Session Mode at Virgin Active.
attendance_priority: First prize: Gym on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday
Second: Still three gym days — makeup Tuesday or Thursday if you miss a plan day
Last resort: Home only if getting to Virgin Active is truly impossible for that session

session_tips.title: What 'hard' means today
session_tips.body: Max results ≠ sloppy grinding. Last hard sets should leave about 0–2 clean reps (pack 04). If you could do 5 more easy reps, the set was too light.

session_tips.title: Why 3 days still wins for you
session_tips.body: Weekly hard sets drive hypertrophy more than training more days (Schoenfeld frequency metas, pack 02). Pack volume into 3 long sessions; 4–5× historically backfires for you.

session_tips.title: Body-type training pick
session_tips.body: Your InBody profile (lean, lost muscle, upper-aesthetic goal) maps to progressive hypertrophy RT with upper specialization + leg maintenance — not cardio-first or random machine hopping (packs 01/03/04).

session_tips.title: Food around training
session_tips.body: Hit ~120–135 g protein today. Small meal 1–2 h before helps hard sessions. Creatine 5 g daily (pack 05/06).

session_tips.title: Weights in kilograms
session_tips.body: Write every working weight. Next session: same reps + more kg, or same kg + more reps.

exercise_library.session_sources_note: Plan C cites Wave-1 packs 01–07 in data/sources/.

overall_tips.title: Best time of day
overall_tips.body: For muscle size over weeks, morning and evening training are about equal when weekly hard sets are matched. A 2019 meta-analysis (Grgic et al., Chronobiology International) found similar hypertrophy morning vs evening (effect size near zero). Pick the time you will actually arrive - that beats the "perfect" clock.

overall_tips.title: Food around training
overall_tips.body: Daily protein (~115-130 g for you) matters more than a magic 30-minute window. For this restart: a small meal or snack 1-2 hours before usually feels better than going completely empty (less light-headedness). If you train fasted and feel fine, that is OK - just hit protein across the day (ISSN protein position stand).

overall_tips.title: How hard (reps left in reserve)
overall_tips.body: Week 1: stop each set when you could still do about 2-3 more clean reps. You do not need every set to failure. Training near failure is enough for growth; grinding every set to failure is not required (failure vs non-failure reviews).

overall_tips.title: Weights in kilograms
overall_tips.body: Virgin Active plates and dumbbells are in kilograms. Write the number on your phone notes: Set 1 = X kg. Next set, try the same or +2.5 kg if it felt easy.

overall_tips.title: Showing up beats perfect
overall_tips.body: First prize: gym Monday / Wednesday / Friday. Second: still three gym days with a makeup weekday. Home only if the gym is truly impossible.

incline_press.where: Adjustable bench + dumbbells (or barbell / Smith incline). Bench about 30-45 degrees.
incline_press.how_to: Set the bench to about 30-45 degrees. Plant your feet flat. Pull your shoulder blades back into the pad. Start with the weights at upper-chest height, elbows about 45 degrees from your torso (not flared flat out). Press up until arms are almost straight without slamming. Lower for about 2 seconds until you feel a stretch across the upper chest.
incline_press.avoid: Bench too upright (turns into a shoulder press). Bouncing the bar/dumbbells off the chest. Huge arch and lifting the hips.
incline_press.set_plan: Set 1: pick X kg for 8–10 reps with 2–3 left | Set 2: same X kg, or X + 2.5 kg if set 1 felt easy | Set 3: X + 2.5 kg only if form stayed smooth — else stay at X
set_plan_lines: Set 1: pick X kg for 8–10 reps with 2–3 left
Set 2: same X kg, or X + 2.5 kg if set 1 felt easy
Set 3: X + 2.5 kg only if form stayed smooth — else stay at X

rom_cues.question: Full stretch at the bottom?
rom_cues.answer: Yes — lower until you feel an upper-chest stretch (~2 sec). Do not bounce.
rom_cues.why: Controlled stretch under load is useful for chest growth; slamming or tiny half-reps waste the set.

tips: Remember: control the lowering - that stretch is where a lot of the chest work happens.
Remember: keep shoulder blades pinned; do not shrug into your ears.
If you only find a barbell incline machine, use that - same idea.

tips_ranked.text: control the lowering - that stretch is where a lot of the chest work happens.
keep shoulder blades pinned; do not shrug into your ears.
If you only find a barbell incline machine, use that - same idea.

incline_press.research_note: Weekly hard sets on a muscle drive growth more than which machine you pick (Schoenfeld volume dose-response). Upper-chest emphasis from incline is a programming choice for your visual goals - not a magic percentage over flat press.

shoulder_press.display_name: Seated dumbbell or machine shoulder press
aliases: Seated dumbbell or machine shoulder press
shoulder_press.where: Seated bench with back support + dumbbells, or a shoulder-press machine.
shoulder_press.how_to: Sit tall with your back against the pad. Start with dumbbells at about ear height. Press overhead until arms lock softly. Lower only to ear height or the machine start position; do not drop into a relaxed dead hang at the bottom. Do not shrug. On a machine: same path, slow and controlled.
shoulder_press.avoid: Big lower-back arch. Pressing the weights far out in front of your face. Dropping into a loose hang between reps.
shoulder_press.set_plan: Set 1: X kg for 8–10 reps with 2–3 left | Set 2: same X kg, or X + 2.5 kg if clean | Set 3: same rule — add 2.5 kg only if set 2 was clean
set_plan_lines: Set 1: X kg for 8–10 reps with 2–3 left
Set 2: same X kg, or X + 2.5 kg if clean
Set 3: same rule — add 2.5 kg only if set 2 was clean

rom_cues.question: Lower to a fully relaxed hang?
rom_cues.answer: No. Stop around ear height (or the machine’s start). Soft lockout at the top — no slam.
rom_cues.why: A dead hang dumps tension, stresses the shoulder joint, and turns the next rep into a risky heave. Controlled end-range keeps delts working.

tips: Remember: this builds the front and middle of the shoulder - key for that round look.
Remember: stop the set when form shakes; do not fight ugly reps in week 1.

tips_ranked.text: this builds the front and middle of the shoulder - key for that round look.
stop the set when form shakes; do not fight ugly reps in week 1.

shoulder_press.research_note: Shoulders respond to local weekly volume. Extra hard sets here support your visual shoulder goal without needing a fourth gym day.

lateral_raise.display_name: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
aliases: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
Cable or machine side lateral raise
lateral_raise.where: Dumbbells standing, cable with a handle at your side, or a lateral-raise machine.
lateral_raise.how_to: Soft bend in the elbows. Lead with the elbows and raise arms out to just below shoulder height. Pause briefly. Lower slowly. Prefer clean reps over heavy weight - this is your main "wider shoulders" move.
lateral_raise.avoid: Swinging the body. Shrugging traps. Flinging weights above the head.
lateral_raise.set_plan: Set 1: light X kg (often 4–8) for 12–15 with ~2 left | Set 2: same X, or +2 / +2.5 kg if easy and strict | Set 3: stay strict — if you swing, the weight is too heavy
set_plan_lines: Set 1: light X kg (often 4–8) for 12–15 with ~2 left
Set 2: same X, or +2 / +2.5 kg if easy and strict
Set 3: stay strict — if you swing, the weight is too heavy

rom_cues.question: Raise above shoulder height?
rom_cues.answer: Stop just below shoulder height if traps take over. Soft elbows the whole time.
rom_cues.why: Side delts build the wide look; shrugging shifts work to traps and steals the point of the move.

tips: Remember: stop just below shoulder height if traps take over.
Remember: side delts are a priority for looking broader in shirts - quality beats ego weight.

tips_ranked.text: stop just below shoulder height if traps take over.
side delts are a priority for looking broader in shirts - quality beats ego weight.

lateral_raise.research_note: Specialty volume on a region works because hypertrophy is largely local (sets per muscle). Raising week-to-week volume here is a standard specialization approach.

chest_fly.where: Pec-deck / fly machine, or dual cables at chest height.
chest_fly.how_to: Keep a slight bend in the elbows and keep that bend fixed. Open until you feel a chest stretch, then bring the arms together as if hugging a tree. Squeeze one second at the inside.
chest_fly.set_plan: Set 1: pin / load for 12–15 smooth reps with ~2 left | Set 2: same pin, or +1 pin if easy | Set 3: stay controlled — light is correct
set_plan_lines: Set 1: pin / load for 12–15 smooth reps with ~2 left
Set 2: same pin, or +1 pin if easy
Set 3: stay controlled — light is correct

rom_cues.question: Open until you feel a chest stretch?
rom_cues.answer: Yes — fixed elbow bend, open to stretch, hug together, squeeze 1 sec inside.
rom_cues.why: Flys top up chest volume; ego weight turns them into a front-delt swing.

tips: Remember: this is a control / pump move - light is correct.
Remember: feel the stretch; do not bounce.

tips_ranked.text: this is a control / pump move - light is correct.
feel the stretch; do not bounce.

chest_fly.research_note: Isolation work adds local volume. It does not replace your main presses - it tops up chest work inside the time budget.

alternatives.name: Skip if short on time
alternatives.when: Pressed for time — keep presses + laterals

pin_guidance: Technogym pec-deck / cable: start mid-stack where 12–15 leave ~2 reps
Next set: +1 pin only if set 1 was easy
Log the pin number on your phone (provisional until we calibrate your stack)

aliases: Barbell or DB curl + pushdown supersets optional
triceps_pushdown.where: Cable stack with a rope or straight / angled bar.
triceps_pushdown.how_to: Pin elbows to your sides. Push down until arms straighten. If using a rope, spread the ends apart at the bottom. Let the forearms come up only as far as the elbows stay still.
triceps_pushdown.avoid: Leaning and turning it into a chest press. Elbows flaring wide.
triceps_pushdown.set_plan: Set 1: pin / load for 10–15 with ~2 left | Set 2: same, or +1 pin if clean | Set 3: elbows glued — only forearms move
set_plan_lines: Set 1: pin / load for 10–15 with ~2 left
Set 2: same, or +1 pin if clean
Set 3: elbows glued — only forearms move

rom_cues.question: Full straighten at the bottom?
rom_cues.answer: Yes — soft lockout and squeeze. Up only as far as elbows stay still.
rom_cues.why: Triceps finish presses; half-reps leave stimulus on the table.

tips: Remember: only the forearms should move - elbows stay glued.
Remember: full straighten at the bottom for the triceps squeeze.

tips_ranked.text: only the forearms should move - elbows stay glued.
full straighten at the bottom for the triceps squeeze.

triceps_pushdown.research_note: Arms get work from presses too; this finishes triceps without long sessions.
pin_guidance: Cable stack: pick a pin for smooth 10–15 with elbows pinned
+1 pin next set only if easy
Prefer rope or angled bar — log pin #

leg_press_or_goblet.display_name: Leg press or goblet squat (or walking lunges)
aliases: Walking lunges OR leg press light
Leg press or goblet squat (or walking lunges)
leg_press_or_goblet.where: Leg-press sled, or one dumbbell for goblet squat, or open floor for walking lunges.
leg_press_or_goblet.how_to: Leg press: feet mid-platform, lower until thighs are about 90 degrees, press without slamming the locks. Goblet squat: hold one dumbbell at your chest, sit between your heels, stand tall. Lunges: short steps, torso upright. These are maintenance only - leave 2-3 reps in reserve.
leg_press_or_goblet.avoid: Butt peeling off the leg-press pad. Knees collapsing inward.
leg_press_or_goblet.set_plan: Set 1: load for 10–12 controlled reps (leave 2–3) | Set 2: same load — do not chase a leg PR this block | Set 3: same load, stop early if knees complain
set_plan_lines: Set 1: load for 10–12 controlled reps (leave 2–3)
Set 2: same load — do not chase a leg PR this block
Set 3: same load, stop early if knees complain

rom_cues.question: Full depth?
rom_cues.answer: Leg press to ~90° thighs; goblet sit between heels. No butt peel / knee cave.
rom_cues.why: Maintenance volume only — health floor, not a leg specialty day.

tips: Remember: legs stay in the plan for health balance (WHO major-muscle guidance) - not to become a leg specialty.
Remember: stop early if knees hurt; choose the machine that feels best.

tips_ranked.text: legs stay in the plan for health balance (WHO major-muscle guidance) - not to become a leg specialty.
stop early if knees hurt; choose the machine that feels best.

leg_press_or_goblet.research_note: Young adults can maintain muscle with much lower volume than they used to build it (Bickel et al., 2011). A few hard sets keep the floor without stealing time from upper body.
pin_guidance: Start mid-stack where 12-15 smooth reps leave 2 in reserve
Next set: +1 pin only if set 1 was easy
Log the pin number on your phone

aliases: Jump rope OR marching in place
Jump rope OR brisk walk outdoors
conditioning.where: Stair climber, incline treadmill, or jump-rope space.
conditioning.how_to: Easy-moderate pace where you can still talk in short sentences. Stair climber: light hands on the rails, full steps. Incline walk: about 5-8% incline, brisk walk not a sprint. Jump rope: steady rhythm. Use the minutes shown on the card.
conditioning.avoid: All-out intervals that trash tomorrow's lift. Hanging on the stair climber rails and cheating the steps.
conditioning.set_plan: One easy block for the minutes listed — no kilograms | StairMaster Technogym level ~4–6 (provisional week 1) | Or incline walk 5–8% · talk-test OK
set_plan_lines: One easy block for the minutes listed — no kilograms
StairMaster Technogym level ~4–6 (provisional week 1)
Or incline walk 5–8% · talk-test OK

rom_cues.question: All-out intervals?
rom_cues.answer: No. Easy–moderate zone 2. Short sentences while moving.
rom_cues.why: Protect tomorrow’s lift; recomp support comes from consistency + protein, not punishing cardio.

tips: Remember: this supports conditioning and midsection goals; lifting still builds the shoulders.
Remember: if you are exhausted from lifting, shorten this, do not skip the whole gym day.

tips_ranked.text: this supports conditioning and midsection goals; lifting still builds the shoulders.
if you are exhausted from lifting, shorten this, do not skip the whole gym day.

conditioning.research_note: Extra easy cardio is optional support. Your main belly/definition lever in this 3-week restart is lifting + protein + not hard-cutting, not punishing cardio.
conditioning.stairmaster_note: Technogym / Virgin Active provisional. Light hands on rails, full steps.

lat_pulldown.where: Lat-pulldown station, assisted pull-up machine, or a pull-up bar.
lat_pulldown.how_to: Pulldown: start with a long reach, pull elbows toward your hips, bar to upper chest, control the return. Assisted pull-up: same idea with help from the knee pad. Negatives: get the chin over the bar, lower for 3-5 seconds.
lat_pulldown.avoid: Pulling the bar behind the neck. Huge swinging half-reps.
lat_pulldown.set_plan: Set 1: pin / load for 8–10 with 2–3 left | Set 2: same or +1 pin / +5 kg if clean | Set 3: full stretch at the top every rep
set_plan_lines: Set 1: pin / load for 8–10 with 2–3 left
Set 2: same or +1 pin / +5 kg if clean
Set 3: full stretch at the top every rep

rom_cues.question: Bar to chest or behind neck?
rom_cues.answer: To upper chest. Never behind the neck. Think elbows to hips.
rom_cues.why: Behind-neck is riskier; full stretch at top lengthens lats for the V-taper.

tips: Remember: think "elbows to hips," not "hands to bar."
Remember: full stretch at the top every rep.

tips_ranked.text: think "elbows to hips," not "hands to bar."
full stretch at the top every rep.

lat_pulldown.research_note: Back volume supports posture and the V-taper that makes shoulders look wider.
pin_guidance: +1 pin or +5 kg if stack jumps by 5 and form holds

seated_row.where: Seated cable row with a V-handle or straight bar.
seated_row.how_to: Sit tall with a slight knee bend. Start with arms long. Pull the handle to your torso, squeeze the shoulder blades, do not shrug. Return to a full stretch. Keep the torso mostly still.
seated_row.avoid: Rocking with the lower back. Shrugging the traps every rep.
seated_row.set_plan: Set 1: load for 8–10 | Set 2: same or +2.5–5 kg if clean | Set 3: squeeze blades 1 sec — no rock
set_plan_lines: Set 1: load for 8–10
Set 2: same or +2.5–5 kg if clean
Set 3: squeeze blades 1 sec — no rock

rom_cues.question: Full stretch at the start?
rom_cues.answer: Yes — arms long, then pull to torso. Chest proud, no shrug.
rom_cues.why: Rows balance pressing days and protect shoulders while you bias push for visuals.

tips: Remember: chest proud, shoulders down.
Remember: squeeze at the chest for one second.

tips_ranked.text: chest proud, shoulders down.
squeeze at the chest for one second.

seated_row.research_note: Rows balance pressing days and help shoulder health with your pressing volume.
pin_guidance: Progress +1 pin when top of range is easy

rear_delt.display_name: Rear delt fly (dumbbell, cable, or machine)
aliases: Rear delt fly (dumbbell, cable, or machine)
rear_delt.where: Bent-over dumbbells, reverse pec-deck, or cables.
rear_delt.how_to: Soft elbows. Sweep arms out and slightly back - show the rear of the shoulders to the wall behind you. Light weight; feel the burn.
rear_delt.avoid: Turning it into a heavy shrug or row with swing.
rear_delt.set_plan: Set 1: light load for 12–15 | Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg | Set 3: if you only feel traps, go lighter
set_plan_lines: Set 1: light load for 12–15
Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg
Set 3: if you only feel traps, go lighter

rom_cues.question: Heavy row swing OK?
rom_cues.answer: No. Soft elbows, sweep out/back — show rear delts to the wall behind you.
rom_cues.why: Rear delts round the 3D shoulder; swing turns it into a trap shrug.

tips: Remember: this rounds out the shoulder from the back - important for the 3D look.
Remember: if you only feel traps, go lighter.

tips_ranked.text: this rounds out the shoulder from the back - important for the 3D look.
if you only feel traps, go lighter.

rear_delt.research_note: Rear-delt work supports aesthetics and pressing comfort.

face_pull.where: Cable set at upper-chest / face height with a rope.
face_pull.how_to: Pull the rope toward your face / forehead. At the end, rotate so thumbs point roughly toward your ears. Squeeze rear delts and upper back.
face_pull.avoid: Pulling the rope to the belly (that becomes a regular row).
face_pull.set_plan: Set 1: light pin for 12–15 smooth | Set 2: same pin — thumbs toward ears at the end | Set 3: form first — this is prehab + shape
set_plan_lines: Set 1: light pin for 12–15 smooth
Set 2: same pin — thumbs toward ears at the end
Set 3: form first — this is prehab + shape

rom_cues.question: Pull to belly or face?
rom_cues.answer: To face / forehead. Externally rotate at the end (thumbs ~ears).
rom_cues.why: Belly height becomes a row; face + rotation hits rear delts and external rotators.

tips: Remember: this is shoulder-friendly "prehab" and shape work - form first.
Remember: external rotation at the end matters.

tips_ranked.text: this is shoulder-friendly "prehab" and shape work - form first.
external rotation at the end matters.

face_pull.research_note: Helpful accessory for shoulder longevity while you bias pressing for visuals.

curl.how_to: Elbows near your sides. Curl up without swinging the hips. Lower fully. Brief squeeze at the top.
curl.set_plan: Set 1: load for 10–12 | Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg | Set 3: full lower — no cheat swing
set_plan_lines: Set 1: load for 10–12
Set 2: same or +2 / +2.5 kg
Set 3: full lower — no cheat swing

rom_cues.question: Skip the bottom stretch?
rom_cues.answer: No — lower fully, brief squeeze at top. Elbows near sides.
rom_cues.why: Cutting ROM chops biceps stimulus; swing is ego, not growth.

tips: Remember: lower all the way - skipping the bottom chops the set short.
Remember: if you swing, drop the weight.

tips_ranked.text: lower all the way - skipping the bottom chops the set short.
if you swing, drop the weight.

curl.research_note: Direct arm work is optional volume on top of rows/pulldowns.

rdl_or_leg_curl.display_name: Romanian deadlift or lying leg curl
aliases: Romanian deadlift OR lying leg curl
Romanian deadlift or lying leg curl
rdl_or_leg_curl.where: Barbell or dumbbell Romanian deadlift, or lying/seated leg-curl machine.
rdl_or_leg_curl.how_to: Romanian deadlift: soft knees, push hips back, weights close to the legs, feel hamstrings stretch, stand by driving hips forward. Leg curl: pad on Achilles, curl heels toward glutes, control down. Light-moderate - hinge maintenance.
rdl_or_leg_curl.avoid: Rounding the upper back hard. Yanking the leg curl.
rdl_or_leg_curl.set_plan: Set 1: load for 8–12 (maintenance) | Set 2: same load — not a max deadlift day | Set 3: hips back, weights close to legs
set_plan_lines: Set 1: load for 8–12 (maintenance)
Set 2: same load — not a max deadlift day
Set 3: hips back, weights close to legs

rom_cues.question: Round the back to go deeper?
rom_cues.answer: No. Soft knees, hips back, flat-ish back, feel hamstrings. Leg curl: control down.
rom_cues.why: Posterior-chain floor for health while upper body stays the priority.

tips: Remember: hips go back like closing a car door with your hips.
Remember: this is not a max deadlift day.

tips_ranked.text: hips go back like closing a car door with your hips.
this is not a max deadlift day.

rdl_or_leg_curl.research_note: Keeps posterior-chain coverage for the WHO-style major-muscle floor while upper body stays the priority.

core_crunch.display_name: Cable crunch or hanging knee raise
aliases: Cable crunch OR hanging knee raise
Cable crunch or hanging knee raise
core_crunch.where: Kneeling cable crunch, or pull-up bar for hanging knee raise.
core_crunch.how_to: Cable crunch: kneel, rope at shoulders or behind head, crunch ribs toward pelvis - do not yank with arms. Hanging knee raise: dead hang, tip the pelvis, raise knees toward the chest under control.
core_crunch.avoid: Swinging like a gymnast kip. Pulling the rope with arms only.
core_crunch.set_plan: Set 1: controlled 10–15 (cable pin = guide) | Set 2: ribs to pelvis — do not yank with arms | Set 3: same control
set_plan_lines: Set 1: controlled 10–15 (cable pin = guide)
Set 2: ribs to pelvis — do not yank with arms
Set 3: same control

rom_cues.question: Yank the rope with arms?
rom_cues.answer: No. Crunch ribs toward pelvis. Hanging raise: tip pelvis, knees up controlled.
rom_cues.why: Abs are for control; midsection look still mostly fat + overall training.

tips: Remember: think "ribs to pelvis," not "pull the rope."
Remember: abs show more from body fat + overall training than from endless crunches alone.

tips_ranked.text: think "ribs to pelvis," not "pull the rope."
abs show more from body fat + overall training than from endless crunches alone.

core_crunch.research_note: Core work is fine for feeling and control; your midsection look still depends mostly on overall fat loss rate and genetics - and you are already near ~17% body fat.

elevated_pushup.how_to: Hands under shoulders (closer together for a diamond push-up). Body straight. Lower the chest toward the hands, press up. Hands elevated = easier. Feet elevated = harder.
elevated_pushup.set_plan: Set 1: match card reps — elevate hands if needed | Set 2: body in one line head to heels | Set 3: full ROM, no sag
set_plan_lines: Set 1: match card reps — elevate hands if needed
Set 2: body in one line head to heels
Set 3: full ROM, no sag

rom_cues.question: Tiny half-reps OK?
rom_cues.answer: No. Chest toward hands, full press. Raise hands to make easier — not uglier form.
rom_cues.why: Home stand-in for pressing when gym is impossible.

tips: Remember: body in one line from head to heels.
Remember: this is the home stand-in for pressing.

tips_ranked.text: body in one line from head to heels.
this is the home stand-in for pressing.

elevated_pushup.research_note: Home volume counts when gym is impossible - still second-best to the gym priority ladder.

pike_pushup.how_to: Hips high in an inverted V. Bend elbows and lower the head toward the floor between the hands, then press back up. This copies a shoulder-press pattern at home.
pike_pushup.avoid: Letting the hips drop into a normal push-up.
pike_pushup.set_plan: Set 1: strict pike reps for the card range | Set 2: hips stay high — inverted V | Set 3: elevate feet later only if easy
set_plan_lines: Set 1: strict pike reps for the card range
Set 2: hips stay high — inverted V
Set 3: elevate feet later only if easy

rom_cues.question: Let hips drop into a normal push-up?
rom_cues.answer: No. Keep hips high; head toward floor between hands.
rom_cues.why: This is the home shoulder-press pattern.

tips: Remember: hips stay high.
Remember: this is your home shoulder builder.

tips_ranked.text: hips stay high.
this is your home shoulder builder.

pike_pushup.research_note: Home shoulder pattern when dumbbells are not available.

alternatives.name: Seated DB press if DBs appear

bw_squat.how_to: Feet about shoulder width. Sit down and slightly back, knees track over toes, stand tall. Split squat: one foot forward, drop the back knee toward the floor.
bw_squat.avoid: Heels lifting. Knees caving hard inward.
bw_squat.set_plan: Set 1: smooth controlled reps for listed sets | Set 2: heels down, knees track toes | Set 3: backpack load if bodyweight is too easy
set_plan_lines: Set 1: smooth controlled reps for listed sets
Set 2: heels down, knees track toes
Set 3: backpack load if bodyweight is too easy

rom_cues.question: Cut depth to make it easy?
rom_cues.answer: Sit down and slightly back with control. Split squat: back knee toward floor.
rom_cues.why: Leg maintenance only at home — health floor, not specialty.

tips: Remember: this is only leg maintenance at home.
Remember: hold a backpack for load if bodyweight is too easy.

tips_ranked.text: this is only leg maintenance at home.
hold a backpack for load if bodyweight is too easy.

bw_squat.research_note: Minimum lower-body stimulus when you cannot reach the gym.

extra_whoop_days.note: WHOOP workout outside scheduled MWF — possible makeup / golf / other

plan_adherence.interpretation: whoop_logged=true means some WHOOP workout exists that calendar day — not proof the exact lifts were done. Use with Dylan's report / portal.

food.disclaimer: Preference list for Dylan — not medical advice. Kosher status can change by batch/factory; check the hechsher on the pack. Availability varies (Dis-Chem, Pick n Pay, Checkers, Wellness Warehouse, Virgin Active cafe).
food.voice_memo_note: Record on Food tab → Export for Cursor → save into data/context/food_voice_inbox/. Coach processes + deletes inbox audio on every chat.

bars.kosher_note: Often OU / local hechsher — verify pack
bars.notes: Easy grab; watch sugar on flavoured variants

bars.brand: USN Protein Delite / similar USN bars
bars.notes: Common in SA gyms; taste varies a lot by flavour

bars.kosher_note: Many are vegetarian; kosher only if marked
bars.notes: Lower protein — snack, not a shake replacement

bars.kosher_note: WW often marks Beth Din / OU — read the label
bars.notes: Convenient; protein per bar varies — aim to stack toward daily band

shakes.kosher_note: Selected flavours carry kosher marks — verify
shakes.notes: Default SA whey workhorse; mix with water or milk
Often well priced; stick to one flavour you will finish
Widely stocked; isolate if lactose bothers you

shakes.kosher_note: Often easier for kosher shoppers — still check
shakes.notes: Use if dairy whey is off the table

shakes.kosher_note: Rarely all kosher — only buy marked cans
shakes.notes: Emergency option after training — expensive per gram

practical_rules: Hit 115–130 g protein most days; bars/shakes are tools, not the whole diet.
Prefer powder over bars for cost and control.
If a pack has no hechsher and you keep kosher strictly, skip it — do not guess.
Creatine 5 g daily stays separate from protein shakes (can mix in the same drink).

coach.tone_rule: Be honest. Impressed when earned; not impressed when behind. Suggest the 2 km walk to gym + 2 km back when adherence is weak.

trust.message: Plan C cites data/sources/01–07. Follow the portal session; ask Cursor for adjustments.
